label: rollout-1
text:
To solve this problem, we need to determine how long it takes for the second train to catch up to the first train after the second train departs. Let's break this down step by step.

### Step 1: Determine the head start of the first train
The first train leaves 2 hours before the second train. Since the first train travels at 60 mph, in those 2 hours, it covers:
\[ 60 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 120 \text{ miles} \]

### Step 2: Set up the relative speed
The second train travels at 90 mph, while the first train travels at 60 mph. The relative speed at which the second train is closing the gap is:
\[ 90 \text{ mph} - 60 \text{ mph} = 30 \text{ mph} \]

### Step 3: Determine the time to catch up
The second train needs to cover the 120-mile head start at a relative speed of 30 mph. The time \( t \) it takes to catch up is given by:
\[ t = \frac{\text{distance}}{\text{relative speed}} = \frac{120 \text{ miles}}{30 \text{ mph}} = 4 \text{ hours} \]

### Final Answer
The second train will catch up to the first train 4 hours after it departs.
